revision.text: #redirect[[en:Weschati-Bekati]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33-N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati-bekati
''Wš3.tj-bk3.tj''
}} '''Weschati-bekati''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Im [[Neues Reich|Neuen Reich]] wurde dieser Dekan in die zwei Einzeldekane Weschati und Bekati unterteilt, nachdem im [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleren Reich]] beide Dekane noch vereint waren. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei der bläulich-weiße [[Beta Centauri|Hadar]] im Sternbild [[Zentaur (Sternbild)|Zentaur]]. Die Sethos-Schrift führte in den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] Weschati als siebten und Bekati als achten Dekan; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] erschien Weschati-bekati als sechster Dekan, dessen [[heliakisch]]er Aufgang für den [[Schemu#Datierungen|26. Schemu II]] angesetzt war. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 87. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati
''Wš3.tj''
|NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati
''Bk3.tj''
}} '''Weschati-Bekati''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Dieser Dekan war in die zwei untergeordneten Einzeldekane Weschati und Bekati geteilt. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei der bläulich-weiße [[Beta Centauri|Hadar]] im Sternbild [[Zentaur (Sternbild)|Zentaur]]. In den Dekanlisten der Sethos-Schrift repäsentierten Weschati sowie Bekati am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den sechsten Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Schemu#Datierungen|26. Schemu II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 87. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:ウェシャティ・ベカティ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Weschati-Bekati]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati

This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. ''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. ''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri|12=draft}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus|12=draft}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Weschati-Bekati]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:ウェシャティ・ベカティ]] [[en:draft:Weschati-Bekati]] [[de:Weschati-Bekati]] [[sn:Weschati-Bekati]] [[en:Weschati-Bekati]] [[sn:eschati-Bekati]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Weschati-Bekati}}

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|de|Beta Centauri|qq=draft}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus (constellation)|de|Zentaur (Sternbild)|lt=Centaurus|qq=draft}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is the bluish-white [[:en:Beta Centauri|Beta Centauri]] (Hadar) in the [[:en:Centaurus|Centaurus]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1339483]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is the bluish-white [[:en:Beta Centauri|Beta Centauri]] (Hadar) in the [[:en:Centaurus|Centaurus]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is the bluish-white [[:en:Beta Centauri|Beta Centauri]] (Hadar) in the [[:en:Centaurus|Centaurus]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is the bluish-white [[:en:Beta Centauri|Beta Centauri]] (Hadar) in the [[:en:Centaurus|Centaurus]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Weschati-Bekati|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:ウェシャティ・ベカティ]] [[de:Weschati-Bekati]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q1339483}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[de:Weschati-Bekati]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1339483]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = G43-M8:t*Z1*Z1 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Weschati
''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is the bluish-white [[:en:Beta Centauri|Beta Centauri]] (Hadar) in the [[:en:Centaurus|Centaurus]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. ''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is the bluish-white [[:en:Beta Centauri|Beta Centauri]] (Hadar) in the [[:en:Centaurus|Centaurus]] constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=26th Schemu II|de|26. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Weschati-Bekati|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:ウェシャティ・ベカティ]] [[de:Weschati-Bekati]] {{wikidata link|Q1339483}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Weschati-Bekati|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:ウェシャティ・ベカティ]] [[de:Weschati-Bekati]] {{wikidata link|Q1339483}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Weschati-Bekati|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:ウェシャティ・ベカティ]] [[de:Weschati-Bekati]] {{wikidata link|Q1339483}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''Wš3.tj'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Weschati |NAME2 = D58-D28:t*Z4 N14-N33 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = Bekati
''Bk3.tj'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Bekati }} '''Weschati-Bekati''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). This decan was divided into two subordinate individual decans: Weschati and Bekati. The most prominent {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}} associated with this decan is the bluish-white {{ill|Beta Centauri|en|Beta Centauri|WD=Q13175|lt=Beta Centauri}} (Hadar) in the {{ill|Centaurus|en|Centaurus|WD=Q8844|lt=Centaurus}} constellation. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Weschati and Bekati represented the sixth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 87. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''.
